prompt_queue.prompt: Maintenance pass for technical, developer-facing living documentation that helps developers understand, use, and extend the code in the repository. Review the repository source code, generated outputs, tests, existing docs, open and recently closed issues, and open and recently closed pull requests as source evidence for documentation gaps, drift, fragmentation, unclear developer guidance, and documentation information architecture problems. Treat existing Future coverage, deferred coverage, out-of-scope, and follow-up sections as a documentation backlog. When source evidence is available and scope fits the run budget, replace deferred bullets with written documentation pages or sections, then remove the satisfied deferred items. Keep only genuinely deferred items with clear reasons. When a workspace is provided, inspect and edit documentation through workspace tools, then run workspace_git_status and workspace_git_diff before finishing. Preserve or improve the repository's existing docs taxonomy, index convention, and topic grouping. For source-code GitHub repositories, README.md and docs/ are the default technical documentation surfaces unless the consumer prompt declares a different audience namespace. Keep repository documentation organized as README.md entry points, docs/README.md indexes, focused docs/** topic pages, and plugin README.md pages when plugin-specific developer contracts need local coverage. Prefer focused updates that keep existing docs aligned with current code behavior across repository-native product names, public APIs, generated-output contracts, internal processes, module connections, extension points, lifecycle, data contracts, auth/permissions, errors and failure modes, examples, operational workflows, compatibility boundaries, and software philosophy. For repositories that package agent-facing or tool-facing outputs, keep docs/** reference pages and plugin README.md files current about the generator inputs, output layout, build pipeline, verification contract, and maintenance workflow. Use no_changes when existing docs are accurate, sufficiently broad for the changed or inspected areas, future/deferred coverage has no bounded item ready to document, and the docs are easy to navigate.
